steps.title: Fastest path: mark the relationships first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships first: equal angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are organized.

steps.title: Recognize AA similarity criterion
steps.content: We have ∠A = ∠P = 54°. For AA similarity, two pairs of equal angles are sufficient. We need to find one more matching angle pair.

steps.title: Test Choice C — The measures of angle B and angle R are 38° and 88°, respectively.
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing Choice C — The measures of angle B and angle R are 38° and 88°, respectively. (∠B = 38°, ∠R = 88°):

In triangle ABC with ∠A = 54°, ∠B = 38°:
∠C = 180° - 54° - 38° = 88°
Angles: 54°, 38°, 88°

In triangle PQR with ∠P = 54°, ∠R = 88°:
∠Q = 180° - 54° - 88° = 38°
Angles: 54°, 38°, 88°

All angles match ✓

steps.title: Confirm similarity
steps.content: Both triangles have angles 54°, 38°, 88°. With ∠A = ∠P and ∠B = ∠Q, the AA criterion is satisfied, so △ABC ∼ △PQR. That matches Choice C — The measures of angle B and angle R are 38° and 88°, respectively..
